seo.title: Alabama Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Alabama — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license alabama, dental assistant certification alabama, RDA alabama, DANB CDA alabama, dental assistant radiography alabama

board.name: Board of Dental Examiners of Alabama
board.mailing_address: 2229 Rocky Ridge Road, Vestavia Hills, AL 35216-9286

license_types.title: Expanded Duty Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Alabama.

steps.title: Meet Basic Eligibility and Course Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you are at least 17 years old, possess a high school diploma or GED, and, if you plan to perform dental radiography, have completed a board-approved course in dental radiography.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Visit the ABDE Application & Renewal Portal at https://portal.dentalboard.org/, click "New Applicant Registration," and create your account. Then, complete and submit the initial Dental Assistant Registration application online.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all required documents through the ABDE Application & Renewal Portal. This includes proof of age, high school diploma/GED, current CPR certification (BLS for Healthcare Providers), and, if applicable, your dental radiography course completion certificate.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable $100 initial registration fee via the ABDE Application & Renewal Portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: The Alabama Board of Dental Examiners will review your application and submitted documents. Upon approval, your Dental Assistant registration will be issued.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Duty Dental Assistant through the Board of Dental Examiners of Alabama by registration.

faqs.question: Does Alabama require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Alabama requires Expanded Duty Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Alabama.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Alabama?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Duty Dental Assistant through the Board of Dental Examiners of Alabama by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Alabama
hero.dek: To register as a Dental Assistant in Alabama, applicants must submit their initial registration application online through the ABDE Application & Renewal Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the ABDE Application & Renewal Portal at https://portal.dentalboard.org/, click "New Applicant Registration," and follow the prompts to create an account with your email and chosen password.
